{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "ClearSCADA (EcoStruxure Geo SCADA Expert) is an industrial SCADA/telemetry platform that supervises and controls remote field devices and sensors, making it an active element in an Industrial IoT environment. The vulnerability concerns improper authentication in the software itself, placing it at the application layer.",
  "category": "scada platform",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerable product is the Foscam C1 Indoor HD Camera—an IoT device that captures video and is deployed in consumer environments. The command-injection flaw is in the device’s firmware/web interface, i.e., the embedded software on the device layer.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Honeywell Experion PKS is a distributed control system used to monitor and control industrial processes, a purpose-built technology deployed in Industrial IoT environments. The vulnerabilities reside in software modules of the control platform itself (application layer), not in general-purpose IT hardware or infrastructure.",
  "category": "industrial controller",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Suprema BioStar 2 is purpose-built management software for Suprema’s biometric access-control devices (fingerprint/facial readers). These devices are IoT endpoints and the software serves as their orchestration layer. The vulnerability is in the web application, so it maps to the application layer of the IoT ecosystem.",
  "category": "sensors",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Bosch IP Helper is a dedicated utility for discovering and configuring Bosch IP cameras, which are IoT devices. The software is purpose-built for IoT deployment and participates directly in device management, fitting the application layer of the IoT ecosystem.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "GE Proficy HMI/SCADA-CIMPLICITY is purpose-built industrial control software that monitors and controls field devices and sensors, acting as an active element in an Industrial IoT system; the flaw is in its web application component, so it maps to the application layer.",
  "category": "industrial controller",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "The vulnerable products are Schneider Electric EcoStruxure Power Monitoring/SCADA software that serve as supervisory and analytics platforms for connected power devices and sensors in industrial facilities. They are purpose-built for monitoring and controlling IoT/OT equipment, so the CVE directly impacts an active element of an IoT system at the application layer.",
  "category": "energy management platform",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "B. Braun SpaceCom2 is a network-connected medical device used in hospitals; it is purpose-built for IoT and the flaw lies in the device’s firmware lacking signature checks, so it is an IoT device-layer vulnerability.",
  "category": "medical device",
  "sector": "healthcare iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "communication",
  "reason": "The vulnerability affects Eclipse Mosquitto, an MQTT broker that implements the MQTT protocol, which is purpose-built and widely used for machine-to-machine messaging in IoT systems; thus the product is an active functional element in the IoT communication layer.",
  "category": "network infrastructure/gateway",
  "sector": "other"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Thermoscan IP is specialized software used to manage and monitor network-connected temperature data loggers; this software is an active element in an IoT sensing system, and the vulnerability is in its installation folder (application layer).",
  "category": "sensors",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "ABB Panel Builder 600 firmware runs on dedicated industrial HMI panels—embedded devices used in industrial automation networks, fitting the definition of purpose-built IoT hardware. The buffer-overflow flaw is in the device’s firmware (FTP service), so it belongs to the device layer.",
  "category": "industrial_hmi_panel",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "Triangle MicroWorks SCADA Data Gateway is a purpose-built gateway used within industrial control and SCADA environments to interface field devices and supervisory systems; it is a functional element of an Industrial IoT deployment, not a general-purpose application. The flaw is in its trusted-certification feature (software logic), placing it in the application layer.",
  "category": "network infrastructure/gateway",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "Dahua DVRs are purpose-built surveillance edge devices in IoT camera systems; the vulnerability is in the device firmware (hard-coded credentials), so it directly affects the device layer of the IoT ecosystem.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "OctoPrint is purpose-built software for remotely controlling and monitoring network-connected 3D printers, which are physical devices that perform actuation tasks. This places the product squarely inside the IoT ecosystem’s application layer. The vulnerability allows brute-forcing of credentials within that IoT-specific platform, so it is classified as an IoT CVE.",
  "category": "actuators",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "Cisco Webex Desk Hub and TelePresence endpoints are dedicated, network-connected hardware devices running embedded RoomOS/CE software. They act as active functional elements in an IoT setup (smart conferencing equipment) and the flaw is in the device’s firmware/OS, satisfying the IoT-specific rule.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "other"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "communication",
  "reason": "The vulnerable product is Silicon Labs Z/IP Gateway SDK, a software stack that implements the Z-Wave-over-IP protocol used in IoT gateways that bridge Z-Wave end-devices with IP networks. This gateway is a purpose-built IoT communication element; the vulnerability involves malformed Z-Wave command frames affecting protocol handling, placing it squarely in the communication layer of an IoT system.",
  "category": "network infrastructure/gateway",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerable product is a network camera (QCP 200W) whose firmware lacks authentication on its RTSP video stream. This camera is a purpose-built IoT device delivering real-time imaging, and the flaw resides in the device/firmware itself, exposing live images. Therefore it is IoT and maps to the device layer.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerable products are network surveillance cameras running dedicated firmware that operate as physical IoT devices. The flaw is in the camera firmware (device layer) and the cameras are active elements of IoT systems, so this CVE is IoT-specific.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "Siemens CP 1604/1616 are industrial communication processor modules that form an integral, purpose-built element within industrial automation networks (industrial IoT). The CSRF flaw exists in the firmware’s embedded web server, i.e., the device layer of the IoT ecosystem.",
  "category": "network infrastructure/gateway",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "onion-oled-js is a library specifically designed to control the OLED expansion on Onion Omega boards, which are purpose-built IoT development devices; the vulnerable code runs locally on the embedded device itself, making it part of the IoT ecosystem’s device layer.",
  "category": "actuators",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",
  "reason": "UniFi Protect is a dedicated video-surveillance management platform that controls and records IoT security cameras; the vulnerability is in its web application logic, fitting the application layer of an IoT system.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerable product is the firmware of a Vivotek PT7135 IP camera, which is a purpose-built IoT device operating at the device layer; the directory-traversal flaw lies in the embedded firmware’s web interface.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "consumer iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",

  "reason": "Visteon automotive infotainment units are embedded devices deployed in vehicles, a purpose-built IoT context. The vulnerability lies in their firmware-update logic (device layer firmware), making the product an active element in an automotive IoT system.",
  "category": "infotainment system",
  "sector": "automative iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerable items are standalone embedded DVR/camera recorders with dedicated firmware that operate as active nodes in a surveillance IoT system; the flaw is in the device firmware itself, so it clearly concerns the device layer of an IoT ecosystem.",
  "category": "cameras",
  "sector": "smart cities iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "JerryScript is a lightweight JavaScript engine explicitly designed to run on resource-constrained, embedded devices commonly found in IoT products; therefore a vulnerability in this engine directly affects an active element of the IoT device layer.",

  "sector": "consumer iot"

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "device",
  "reason": "The vulnerability affects firmware running on WAGO PFC100/PFC200 programmable logic controllers—purpose-built, embedded industrial devices that act as active elements in industrial IoT deployments. The issue is in the device’s service (iocheckd), i.e., firmware/hardware level, so it belongs to the device layer.",
  "category": "industrial controller",
  "sector": "industrial iot"
}

{
  "label": "IoT",
  "component": "application",

The vulnerability directly affects this IoT element, not generic IT software.",
  "category": "gateway",

}

{
  "label": "nonIoT",

Therefore, it does not meet the criteria for an IoT-specific CVE.",
  "category": "other",

}
